uiLibrary: Vanilla HTML/CSS/JS (teal accent for main site, amber/gold for KG)
orm: supabase-py client (raw table queries, no ORM)
deployment: Render.com (primary) or VPS via systemd+nginx; Docker multi-stage build available
architecture: Single-process: FastAPI web UI + Telegram webhook on one Uvicorn port (10000); polling mode for local dev

tech_stack.settings: Pydantic BaseSettings v2 + pydantic-settings YAML source
tech_stack.mobile_detection: User-Agent regex in website/app.py, redirects to /m/

directory_structure.website/: FastAPI web application — factory in website/app.py
directory_structure.website/app.py: create_app() factory: mounts static assets, registers routes, handles mobile redirects
directory_structure.website/api/routes.py: APIRouter(prefix='/api'): GET /health, GET /graph, POST /summarize; in-memory rate limiter (10 req/min per IP)
directory_structure.website/core/pipeline.py: Stateless URL summarization pipeline reusing bot extractors; no disk writes, no dedup
directory_structure.website/core/graph_store.py: Thread-safe in-memory graph store backed by features/knowledge_graph/content/graph.json
directory_structure.website/features/: Production feature modules — each subfolder is self-contained (HTML+CSS+JS+content)
directory_structure.website/features/knowledge_graph/: 3D knowledge graph viewer; graph.json lives at features/knowledge_graph/content/graph.json
directory_structure.website/experimental_features/: Staging area for features under development before promotion to features/
directory_structure.website/static/: Desktop main page assets (css/style.css, js/app.js, index.html)
directory_structure.website/mobile/: Mobile-optimised pages: index.html, knowledge-graph.html, css/, js/
directory_structure.supabase/: SQL schema files (not Supabase CLI project — manual schema management)
directory_structure.supabase/website/kg_public/schema.sql: Full PostgreSQL schema: kg_users, kg_nodes, kg_links, indexes, RLS policies, views (kg_user_stats, kg_graph_view)
directory_structure.telegram_bot/: Bot layer: config, pipeline, sources (extractors), bot handlers
directory_structure.telegram_bot/config/settings.py: Pydantic Settings singleton via get_settings() (lru_cache); validates required fields on first call
directory_structure.telegram_bot/pipeline/orchestrator.py: Top-level pipeline: redirect -> normalize -> detect -> dedup -> extract -> summarize -> write -> mark seen
directory_structure.telegram_bot/pipeline/summarizer.py: GeminiSummarizer with 3-model fallback cascade; graceful raw-content fallback on total failure
directory_structure.telegram_bot/pipeline/writer.py: Writes Obsidian Markdown notes to KG_DIRECTORY (local mode)
directory_structure.telegram_bot/pipeline/github_writer.py: Pushes notes to GitHub via Contents API (cloud mode, base64 PUT)
directory_structure.telegram_bot/sources/: Plugin-pattern source extractors; auto-discovered via __init__.py scan for SourceExtractor subclasses
directory_structure.telegram_bot/models/capture.py: Shared Pydantic models: SourceType (enum), CaptureRequest, ExtractedContent, ProcessedNote
directory_structure.telegram_bot/main.py: Wires PTB app + FastAPI; webhook mode uses lifespan context for PTB lifecycle
directory_structure.telegram_bot/bot/handlers.py: All Telegram command handlers, delegating to orchestrator.process_url
directory_structure.telegram_bot/bot/guards.py: Chat-ID allow-list filter for bot handlers
directory_structure.ops/Dockerfile: Multi-stage build: python:3.12-slim, exposes port 10000, entry: python run.py
directory_structure.ops/scripts/migrate_graph_to_supabase.py: One-time migration of graph.json to Supabase
directory_structure.tests/: Unit + integration tests; tests/integration_tests/ for live network tests
directory_structure.run.py: CLI entry point, delegates to telegram_bot.main
directory_structure.pyproject.toml: pytest config: asyncio_mode=auto; 'live' marker for real-API tests

existing_patterns.feature_promotion_workflow: Features are developed in website/experimental_features/<feature-name>/ (self-contained: HTML+CSS+JS), then promoted to website/features/<feature-name>/ for production. No build step — plain static files.
existing_patterns.feature_registration: To expose a feature, add StaticFiles mounts and route handlers in website/app.py create_app(). No auto-discovery — manual wiring required.
existing_patterns.supabase_usage: Optional dual-write pattern: every POST /api/summarize writes to both file store (graph.json) and Supabase. GET /api/graph reads from Supabase first with 30s TTL cache, falls back to file store. Supabase is activated by setting SUPABASE_URL + SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY env vars.
existing_patterns.supabase_client_init: Lazy singleton via get_supabase_client() (lru_cache). Credentials read from supabase/.env (loaded with dotenv override=False, so env vars take precedence). Uses SERVICE_ROLE_KEY for server-side operations (bypasses RLS).
existing_patterns.supabase_user_model: kg_users table stores external user IDs in render_user_id TEXT column. Currently uses a single hardcoded 'default-web-user' — designed to be replaced with real auth user IDs.
existing_patterns.api_security: Only IP-based rate limiting (10 req/min per IP, in-memory dict). No authentication on any API endpoint currently. Telegram webhook validates X-Telegram-Bot-Api-Secret-Token header when webhook_secret is set.
existing_patterns.settings_pattern: Pydantic BaseSettings singleton via get_settings() (lru_cache). Priority: constructor args > env vars > .env file > ops/config.yaml. Tests must mock get_settings() to avoid SystemExit(1) from _validate_settings().
existing_patterns.mobile_routing: User-agent detection in website/app.py _is_mobile(). GET / and GET /knowledge-graph redirect mobile browsers to /m/ and /m/knowledge-graph respectively.
existing_patterns.source_extractor_plugin: Subclasses of SourceExtractor in telegram_bot/sources/ are auto-registered. To add a source: add SourceType enum value, create extractor module, add URL pattern to sources/registry.py.

auth_integration_targets: website/app.py — add auth middleware or protected route wrappers in create_app()
website/api/routes.py — add auth dependency to /api/summarize and /api/graph endpoints
website/core/supabase_kg/client.py — already loads supabase/.env; ANON_KEY needed for client-side Supabase auth
website/core/supabase_kg/models.py — KGUser.render_user_id field is the external ID slot for auth provider user IDs
website/core/supabase_kg/repository.py — get_or_create_user() accepts any string render_user_id; wire real auth user IDs here
supabase/website/kg_public/schema.sql — RLS policies already use auth.uid(); schema is auth-ready
website/static/index.html — login UI would hook in here (header area, before main content)
website/static/js/app.js — JS auth flow (Supabase JS client or session token handling)
ops/.env.example — SUPABASE_ANON_KEY is already present as optional var

supabase_auth_readiness.status: Partially ready — schema has auth.uid()-based RLS; backend bypasses RLS with service role key; no actual auth flow exists yet
supabase_auth_readiness.kg_users_render_user_id: The render_user_id TEXT column in kg_users is the intended slot for external auth provider user IDs (named for Render Auth but can hold any provider's user ID string)
supabase_auth_readiness.current_default_user: Routes hardcode 'default-web-user' as render_user_id in _get_supabase() — this is the placeholder to replace with session-authenticated user IDs
supabase_auth_readiness.rls_policies: All three tables (kg_users, kg_nodes, kg_links) have RLS enabled with auth.uid() policies for client-side access AND service_role bypass for server-side

integration_points.where_auth_middleware_goes: FastAPI middleware or Depends() injection in website/api/routes.py — FastAPI's dependency injection is the standard pattern
integration_points.where_login_ui_goes: website/static/index.html header section; a login modal or sidebar component can be added before the <main> block. The header div.logo area is the natural placement.
integration_points.session_token_flow: For Supabase Auth: Supabase JS client (CDN or bundled) in app.js handles sign-in; JWT passed as Authorization: Bearer header to /api/* endpoints; FastAPI dependency verifies JWT using Supabase ANON_KEY
integration_points.graph_scoping: POST /api/summarize and GET /api/graph currently use a single global user. With auth, the authenticated user's UUID replaces 'default-web-user' in _get_supabase() calls, scoping all KG data per user.
integration_points.new_api_routes_pattern: Add new APIRouter or extend existing router in website/api/routes.py. Include router in website/app.py via app.include_router(). Auth endpoints (login, logout, callback) would be a new router e.g. website/api/auth.py.

deployment.render_config: render.yaml (deleted in current working tree — managed via Render dashboard)
deployment.mode: Webhook mode (WEBHOOK_MODE=true) in production; single Uvicorn process serves both web UI and Telegram webhook
deployment.storage_caveat: Render filesystem is ephemeral — graph.json and KG notes do not survive redeploys. Use Supabase for graph persistence; use GITHUB_TOKEN+GITHUB_REPO for note persistence.
deployment.docker: ops/Dockerfile — multi-stage python:3.12-slim build, entry: python run.py, exposes 10000
deployment.vps_alternative: ops/deploy/DEPLOY.md — systemd service + nginx reverse proxy + certbot TLS
deployment.keep_alive: .github/workflows/keep-alive.yml pings Render URL every 14 min to prevent cold starts

conventions.naming: snake_case modules/files, PascalCase classes, UPPER_SNAKE env vars
conventions.state_management: No frontend framework — plain DOM manipulation in app.js; show/hide sections with .hidden CSS class
conventions.api_pattern: FastAPI APIRouter under /api prefix; JSON request/response bodies; Pydantic models for validation
conventions.async_pattern: Full async/await throughout pipeline and API handlers; pytest asyncio_mode=auto
conventions.feature_isolation: Each website feature is a self-contained directory with its own HTML+CSS+JS; no shared build step
conventions.settings_injection: get_settings() lru_cache singleton; always mock in tests via @patch
conventions.no_purple: UI accent colors: teal (#20B2AA range) for main site, amber/gold (#D4A024) for knowledge graph — no purple/violet/lavender
conventions.commit_style: Conventional commits: feat:, fix:, refactor:, docs:, test: — no Co-Authored-By trailers
